biography.value:
Anver "Tarsh" Resh — Hardby Broker

The intermediary who hired Merev Sarth. Former Cindren & Vhal Hardby clerk under Solen Mereth (1576–1582), now a freelance broker operating out of the Coopered Wreck tavern. The Phase 3 confrontation node — yields names if cornered with leverage, will not testify, will not name his Rel Astra contact.

Description

Thin, fifties, balding with a fringe of dark hair worn long. A long pale face. Yellow stains on the second and third fingers of his right hand from cheap pipe-leaf. Smoker's rasp in the voice. Plain dark wool, a heavy wool coat in winter, no jewelry. He looks exactly like what he is: a clerk who left the office and never quite stopped clerking.

Combat behavior

Avoids combat. Resh is a broker, not a fighter. He will negotiate, deflect, deceive, and exit before drawing a weapon. If combat starts in the Coopered Wreck, his bodyguard (if present) and a paid pair of harbor-district enforcers nearby will defend him while he exits through Olfard's back room. If he must fight, he uses his hand crossbow at range and his Cunning Action to disengage to cover.

His one rule: he does not kill personally. Has not in sixteen years. Will not now. He hires it done.

Statblock summary (CR 6, PB +3)

AC 14 (studded leather under his coat) · HP 78 (12d8+24) · Speed 30 ft. · Init +6 · Passive Perception 14

STR 10 (+0) · DEX 17 (+3) · CON 13 (+1) · INT 16 (+3) · WIS 14 (+2) · CHA 14 (+2)

Saves: DEX +6, INT +6.

Skills: Deception +5, Insight +5, Investigation +6, Perception +5, Persuasion +5, Sleight of Hand +6, Stealth +6.

Senses: Passive Perception 14. Languages: Common, Old Aerdi, Thieves' Cant.

Traits

  • Cunning Action. Bonus action to Dash, Disengage, or Hide.
  • Sneak Attack (1/Turn). +14 (4d6) damage on a weapon attack with advantage or an ally within 5 ft.
  • Uncanny Dodge. Reaction to halve damage from an attack he can see.
  • Evasion. No damage on a successful Dex save, half on a failed save.
  • Reader of Rooms. Advantage on Insight checks to determine if someone is lying or holding back, in any setting where he is the room's most senior occupant.
  • The Broker's Rule. Resh will not personally kill. He surrenders rather than make a killing blow himself.

Actions

  • Multiattack. Two weapon attacks.
  • Hand Crossbow. Ranged Weapon Attack +6, range 30/120. Hit: 1d6+3 piercing.
  • Hidden Knife. Melee Weapon Attack +6, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d4+3 piercing. He uses this only as last resort.
  • Smoke Pellet (2/encounter). Action to throw a 10-ft cube of acrid smoke at a point within 30 ft. The area is heavily obscured for 1 minute or until dispersed. Each creature inside at the start of its turn must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution save or be Blinded until the start of its next turn.

Equipment

  • Studded leather armor under his greatcoat (concealed).
  • Hand crossbow + 10 bolts (in a courier's tube at his hip).
  • Hidden knife in his right boot.
  • Two smoke pellets in a coat pocket.
  • A small pipe and a worn brass tobacco-tin.
  • A small leather wallet with a notary's stylus and a smaller knife (his old C&V working tools).
  • No coin on his person beyond pocket-money. No identifying papers. No personal jewelry.

Source

Blooming Rot, Part 2 — Phase 3. See Hardby NPCs → Anver Resh and Investigation → Tarsh Confrontation for the scene's full setup.

description.value:
Storage: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.equipment.Item.phbagCaseCrossbo]{Case}

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.equipment.Item.phbagAmmunitionV]{Ammunition} is required by a weapon that has the Ammunition property. A weapon’s description specifies the type of ammunition used by the weapon. The Ammunition table lists the different types and the amount you get when you buy them. The table also lists the item that is typically used to store each type; storage must be bought separately.
Plain dark wool, slightly worn at the cuffs. Conceals the studded leather beneath. Smells faintly of cheap pipe-leaf.

Action: Throw at a point within 30 ft. Creates a 10-ft cube of acrid smoke (heavily obscured) for 1 minute or until dispersed. Each creature inside at the start of its turn must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution save or be Blinded until the start of its next turn.
A small clay pipe and a worn brass tobacco-tin. Cheap pipe-leaf. The yellow stains on his right-hand fingers are from this.

Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Write text with impressive flourishes that guard against forgery (DC 15)

Craft: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.equipment.Item.phbagInk00000000]{Ink}, Spell Scroll

Your quick thinking and agility allow you to move and act quickly. On your turn, you can take one of the following actions as a Bonus Action: &Reference[Dash], &Reference[Disengage], or &Reference[Hide].

You know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe’s distraction. Once per turn, you can deal an extra 1d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack roll if you have Advantage on the roll and the attack uses a Finesse or a Ranged weapon. The extra damage’s type is the same as the weapon’s type.

You don’t need Advantage on the attack roll if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the target, the ally doesn’t have the Incapacitated condition, and you don’t have Disadvantage on the attack roll.

The extra damage increases as you gain Rogue levels, as shown in the Sneak Attack column of the Rogue Features table.

Current Sneak Attack Damage: [[lookup @scale.rogue.sneak-attack]]

Foundry Note

Your sneak attack damage will automatically scale as you level up. You can select the damage type in the damage roll's dialog box.

When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to halve the attack’s damage against you (round down).

Foundry Note

With your token selected you can either right click on the damage roll's chat card and select  Apply Half Damage or click the "1/2" button and then the  Apply button.

activation.condition: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack roll

description.value:
When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail.

You don't benefit from this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition.
Advantage on Insight checks to determine if someone is lying or holding back, in any setting where Anver is the room's most senior occupant.
Anver will not personally kill. Has not in sixteen years. Surrenders rather than make a killing blow himself, even if doing so means giving up a fight he is otherwise winning.
